response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded/C/C++ exposure, but the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep neural network training/inference optimization, GPU architecture work, or hardware-software co-design. The role is a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerators, which requires deep hardware-level expertise that this candidate does not demonstrate.
strengths: Long and varied software engineering career across multiple stacks and domains
Some low-level/systems exposure via C/C++ at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, performance-sensitive backend work
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware accelerator design experience
No demonstrated deep learning training/inference optimization or GPU architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack focused, not hardware-software co-design or chip architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/AI accelerator hardware expertise
No deep learning workload or GPU architecture experience
